evals.prompt: Build me a prospect list of CTOs at SaaS startups and get verified emails for stripe.com.
evals.expected_output: The skill runs the full routed pipeline (company search, people search, Hunter domain emails, Hunter email finder, Hunter email verification, Abstract Company Enrichment) and returns a per-step summary with the prospect data and a deliverability verdict.
assertions: The Hunter step is a POST to https://hunter.mpp.paywithlocus.com/hunter/domain-search with domain in the body
The run respects the top-level maxAmount cap of 1.00

evals.prompt: Get me all the emails at notion.so.
evals.expected_output: The skill runs with domain=notion.so; the Hunter domain-search step returns the emails found at the domain, and the Abstract Company Enrichment step enriches the company.
assertions: The domain param is set to notion.so
The Hunter domain-search step host is hunter.mpp.paywithlocus.com

evals.prompt: Find Sarah Chen's email at stripe.com and verify it.
evals.expected_output: The Hunter email-finder step uses first_name=Sarah, last_name=Chen, domain=stripe.com; the Hunter email-verifier step verifies the resulting address.
assertions: The email-finder step posts to https://hunter.mpp.paywithlocus.com/hunter/email-finder with domain, first_name, and last_name in the body
firstName and lastName params flow into the email-finder body
The verification step runs after the email-finder step

evals.prompt: Find Series A B2B SaaS companies in San Francisco with 50-200 employees and their VP Sales contacts.
evals.expected_output: The Fiber natural-language company search uses the ICP query, and the people search scopes to the supplied job titles and locations.
assertions: The companyQuery param carries the free-form ICP description
jobTitles is passed into the Fiber people-search body

evals.prompt: How much could a full sales-prospecting run cost and which payment rail does it use?
evals.expected_output: Explains that every step is routed MPP via the SELAT Router, per-step caps are ~10x each live price ($0.10–$1.00), and the live prices (probe-verified 2026-07-10) are $0.10815 domain-search, $0.01365 email-finder, $0.0084 email-verifier, and $0.0063 company-enrichment lookup.
assertions: The answer states all steps are routed (MPP via SELAT Router)
The answer cites the 1.00 top-level maxAmount fallback cap as a ceiling, distinct from the live prices

evals.prompt: I want to check the sales-prospecting endpoints are payable before spending anything.
evals.expected_output: Recommends running selat-pay --probe-only against the Fiber, Hunter, and Abstract Company Enrichment endpoints to get the 402 payment requirements without paying.
assertions: Probe commands target the same hosts as the manifest steps
No real payment is made during probing
